questions.question: Find the sum of all the numbers between 150 and 450 which are divisible by 17.
questions.explanation: The least such number = 17*9 = 153 The highest such number = 17*26 = 442 Therefore the required sum = (17*9) + (17*10) +....+ (17*26) = 17*(9+10+...+26) Let x = (9 + 10 + ... + 26) Therefore the required number is 17x. Now, x = 9 + 10 + ... + 26 Here number of terms are 18. 9 + 10 + ... + 26 = 18(1st term+ last term)/2 = 9(9+26) = 315 Hence the required number is 17x = 17*315 = 5355

questions.question: What is the compound interest accrued on an amount of Rs. 18,000 at the rate of 9% per annum at the end of 3 years?
If x = ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p3758/image003.png) , what is the value of (‚ąö3 + x)/(‚ąö3 - x)?
questions.explanation: x = ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p3758/image003.png) => x = ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p3758/image004.png) => x = 1 + 1/(1 + x) => x(1 + x) = 1 + x + 1 => x + x2 = 2 + x => x2 = 2 => x = ‚ąö2 x ‚Č† ‚ąí‚ąö2 as none of the terms in the continued fraction is negative. ‚ąī (‚ąö3 + x)/(‚ąö3 - x) = (‚ąö3 + ‚ąö2)/(‚ąö3 ‚ąí ‚ąö2) = (‚ąö3 + ‚ąö2)(‚ąö3 + ‚ąö2)/(‚ąö3 ‚ąí ‚ąö2)(‚ąö3 + ‚ąö2) = (‚ąö3 + ‚ąö2)2 = 5 + 2‚ąö6

questions.question: If 8x ÷ 42x - 3 = 4, then what is the value of (3x - x3)?
If (sin600 + sec300)2 + (cos450 + cosec450)2 = p + sin300 + (1/12), then what is the value of 'p'?
A ‚Ė≥ABC is inscribed in a circle whose centre is O. ‚ą†OAC = 250, then what is the measure of ‚ą†ABC?
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p3792/image013.jpg) In ‚Ė≥AOC, ‚ą†OAC = ‚ą†OCA= 250 [OA = OC = Radius] ‚ą†AOC = 1800 - (‚ą†OCA + ‚ą†OAC) = 1800 - (250 + 250) = 1300 ‚ą†ABC = ‚ą†AOC/2 [The angle subtended by an arc at the centre is double the angle subtended by it at any point on the remaining part of the circle.] = 1300/2 = 650

questions.question: In a triangle MNO, ‚ą†NMO = 500, then find the measure of ‚ą†NPO if NP and OP are angle bisector of ‚ą†MNO and ‚ą†MON respectively.
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p3792/image019.png) Since, NP and OP are angle bisector of ‚ą†MNO and ‚ą†MON respectively. ‚ą†PNO = ‚ą†MNO/2 => ‚ą†MNO = 2 * ‚ą†PNO ‚ą†PON = ‚ą†MON/2 => ‚ą†MON = 2 * ‚ą†PON In ‚Ė≥MNO, ‚ą†NMO + ‚ą†MNO + ‚ą†MON = 1800 500 + ‚ą†MNO + ‚ą†MON = 1800 ‚ą†MNO + ‚ą†MON = 1800 - 500 = 1300 2 * ‚ą†PNO + 2 * ‚ą†PON = 1300 ‚ą†PNO + ‚ą†PON = 1300/2 = 650 ...... (1) In ‚Ė≥PNO, ‚ą†NPO + ‚ą†PNO + ‚ą†PON = 1800 From equation (1)- ‚ą†NPO + 650 = 1800 ‚ą†NPO = 1800 - 650 = 1150

questions.question: A bag contains 750 mangoes, out of which 14% are red in colour, 24% are yellow in colour and rest are green in colour. Find the number of green coloured mangoes.
questions.explanation: Percentage of green coloured mangoes = (100 - 14 - 24) = 62% Number of green coloured mangoes = 62/100 x 750 = 465

questions.question: If x + (1/x) = 4, what is the value of x5 + (1/x5)?
If cos2x = 4sinx, what is the value of cosec x?
questions.explanation: cos2x = 4sinx => 1 - sin2x = 4sinx => sin2x + 4sin x - 1 = 0 => sin2x + 2*sin x*2 + 22 - 22 - 1 = 0 => (sin x + 2)2 = 5 => sin x + 2 = ¬Ī‚ąö5 If sin x + 2 = ‚ąí‚ąö5 sin x = ‚ąí 2 ‚ąí ‚ąö5 [Not possible as ‚ąí1‚Č§sin x‚Č§1] ‚ąī sin x = ‚ąö5 - 2 ‚ąī cosec x = 1/(‚ąö5 - 2) = (‚ąö5 + 2)/(‚ąö5 - 2)(‚ąö5 + 2) = ‚ąö5 + 2

questions.question: If A + B = 90¬į, what is the simplified value of (cos3A + cos B)/(1 + tan A + cot3B)?
questions.explanation: A + B = 90¬į ‚ąī cos B = sin A and tan A = cot B cos3A + cos B = cos3A + sin A = cos3A(1 + sin A/cos3 A) = cos3A(1 + (1/cos2A)(sin A/cos A)) = cos3A(1 + sec2A tan A) = cos3A(1 + (1 + tan2A)tan A) = cos3A(1 + tan A + tan3A) = cos3A(1 + tan A + cot3B) ‚ąī (cos3A + cos B)/(1 + tan A + cot3B) = cos3A

questions.question: In a parallelogram ABCD, perpendicular distance between sides AB and CD is 10 cm while that of between sides AD and BC is 15 cm and area of ABCD is 240 cm2, then what is the difference between length of sides AB and AD?
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p3879/image006.jpg) AP = 10 cm and AQ = 15 cm Area of ABCD = AB * AP = AD * AQ 240 = AB * 10 = AD * 15 AB = 24 cm AD = 16 cm Required difference = 24 - 16 = 8 cm

questions.question: Total monthly allowance of Pawan is what percent of his total monthly basic salary?
questions.explanation: Total monthly basic salary of Pawan = Rs.24000 Total monthly allowance of Pawan = 36000 - 24000 = Rs.12000 Required percentage = (12000/24000) * 100 = 50%

questions.question: Total monthly basic salary of Santosh is how much more than the total monthly allowance of Sushil?
questions.explanation: Total monthly basic salary of Santosh = 60% of 45000 = Rs.27000 Total monthly allowance of Sushil = 30000 - 18000 = Rs.12000 Required amount = 27000 - 12000 = Rs.15000

questions.question: What is the ratio of total Monthly allowance of Pankaj to the total monthly basic salary of Sushil?
questions.explanation: Total Monthly allowance of Pankaj = 25% of 32000 = Rs.8000 Total monthly basic salary of Sushil = Rs.18000 Required ratio = 8000: 18000 = 4: 9

questions.question: Total monthly allowance of all the four employees together is what part of total monthly salary of Sushil and Santosh together?
questions.explanation: Total monthly allowance of all the four employees together = (25% of 32000) + (30000 - 18000) + (40% of 45000) + (36000 - 24000) = 8000 + 12000 + 18000 + 12000 = Rs.50000 Total monthly salary of Sushil and Santosh together = 30000 + 45000 = Rs.75000 Required answer = 50000/75000 = 2/3

questions.question: If 4x3 - 5x2 + 6x - 81 is divisible by (x - k), then what is the value of K?
questions.explanation: Since, 4x3 - 5x2 + 6x - 81 is divisible by (x - k) => x = k must satisfy the given equation => 4k3 - 5k2 + 6k - 81 = 0 => 4k3 - 12k2 + 7k2 - 21k + 27k - 81 = 0 => 4k2(k - 3) + 7k(k - 3) + 27(k - 3) = 0 => (k - 3)(4k2 + 7k + 27) = 0 => k = 3 , k = (-7) ¬Ī ‚ąö(49 - 432)/8 => k = 3 and remaining values are imaginary

questions.question: What is the equation of line which makes 300 from y-axis and passes through point (0, ‚ąö3)?
questions.explanation: Equation of line- y = mx + c Where, m = slope or tangent of angle from y-axis and c = y intercept m = tan300 = 1/‚ąö3 Required equation of line- y = (x/‚ąö3) + c Since, this line passes through (0, ‚ąö3). So, this point must satisfy the equation of line. (‚ąö3) = (0/‚ąö3) + c c = ‚ąö3 Now, the equation of line- y = (x/‚ąö3) + ‚ąö3 ‚ąö3y = (x + 3) x - ‚ąö3y + 3 = 0

questions.question: Train-A covered a distance of 2320 km in 16 hours. Speed of Train-B is four-fifth of the speed of the Train-A. How much distance will Train-C cover in 8 hours if the speed of the train-C is three-fourth of the speed of the Train-B?
questions.explanation: Speed of train A = 2320/16 = 145 km/hour Speed of train B = 145(4/5) = 116 km/hour Speed of train C = 116(3/4) = 87 km/hour Distance covered in 8 hours by train C = 87 X 8 = 696 km Option 2

questions.question: A and B can complete a task in 28 days. B and C can complete it in 56 days and A and C can complete it in 84 days. In how many days will A complete it working separately? (approximately)
questions.explanation: One day's work of A and B = 1/28 One day's work of B and C = 1/56 One day's work of A and C = 1/84 2(One day's work of A, B and C) = 1/28 + 1/56 + 1/84 = 11/168 One day's work of A, B and C = 11/336 One day's work of A = (One day's work of A, B and C) - (One day's work of B and C) = 11/336 - 1/56 = 5/336 Hence A will complete the task in 336/5 = 67.2 ¬Ľ 67 days

questions.question: The average age of 10 candidates in an exam hall is decreased by 4 months when two of them aged 15 years and 17 years are replaced by two new candidates. The average age of new candidates is:
questions.explanation: Decrease = ( 10 X 4 ) months = 40 months = 3 yr. 4 months ‚ąī Total age of new students = (15 + 17) yrs. - (3 yr. 4 months) = 28 years 8months. Average age of new students = 14 years 4 months. Hence, option 2.

questions.question: A shopkeeper bought a shirt for Rs 560 and marked it 70% higher than its price. He offered 25% discount on the marked price. A regular customer asked for a further reduction of Rs 60 and purchased the shirt. Find the percent profit the shopkeeper made by selling the shirt.
questions.explanation: MP = 1.7 x 560 = Rs 952 Price after 25% discount = 952 x 0.75 = Rs 714 Selling price = 714 - 60 = Rs 654 Profit % = (654-560)/560 x 100 = 94/560 x 100 = 16.78%

questions.question: Amish marked the price of a toy up by 20%. He gave a discount of 10% and sold it for Rs 1879.20. Had he sold it for Rs 1513.80, what would have been his loss %?
Two boxes have the same height equal to 35 cm. One box has a square base of side 10 cm. The other box is cylindrical and the diameter of its base is 12 cm. What is the difference in their volumes?
questions.explanation: Required Difference = Volume of cylinder - volume of cuboid = πr2h - lbh = 22/7 x 6 x 6 x 35 - 35 x 10 x 10 = 3960 - 3500 = 460 cm3

questions.question: The ratio of the present age of Smitu and her son is 7:3. 't' years back the sum of their ages added up to be 48 while the ratio of their ages then was 3:1. What shall be the ratio of their ages after another '2t' years?
questions.explanation: Ratio of the ages of Smitu and her son = 7:3. Let their age be 7x and 3x respectively. 't' years ago, (7x - t)/(3x - t) = 3 or 7x - t = 9x - 3t or 2t = 2x or t = x. Sum of their ages, 't' years ago is 10x - 2t = 48 Substituting the value of t = x, we get 8x = 48 or x = 6. Thus t = 6. Present age of Smitu and her son is 42 and 18 respectively. Their ages after '2t' years, or 12 years, shall be 54 and 30 respectively. Thus ratio of their ages shall be 9:5.
